expected_observations.bean_age_nearly_unchanged: Bean age posterior (fresh ~0.73) barely shifts from prior (0.70) because freshness only affects watery, which is already explained by extraction level
expected_observations.fine_grind_most_likely: Fine grind rises from prior 0.30 to ~0.57 because fine grind + any temperature strongly favors over-extraction (0.25-0.80 probability of over across temperatures)
expected_observations.freshness_tracks_bean_age: Freshness posterior closely tracks bean_age posterior because freshness is directly determined by bean_age in the model
expected_observations.high_temp_increases: High temperature rises from prior 0.15 to ~0.28 because high temp drives over-extraction, but optimal temperature remains most likely at ~0.64 due to its high prior (0.70)
expected_observations.over_extraction_dominant: Over-extraction posterior is ~0.89, explaining bitter=yes (P=0.85), sour=no (P=0.90), and watery=no simultaneously. Under-extraction is virtually ruled out (0.001) because it strongly predicts sour=yes and watery=yes.

notes.description: Golden expected output for the Coffee Shop Espresso Diagnosis Bayesian inference scenario. Posteriors computed via exact variable elimination over the 8-variable BIF model given evidence bitter=yes, sour=no, watery=no. Over-extraction is overwhelmingly the most likely cause of bitter, not-sour, not-watery espresso.

gibbs_sampling.note: Stochastic MCMC sampling. Over-extraction is so dominant that Gibbs should converge quickly. With 10000 samples, posteriors should be within 0.05 of exact values.

junction_tree.note: Should match VE posteriors exactly (both are exact inference methods)

loopy_bp.note: Approximate message-passing. The extraction variable has a strong signal, so LBP should converge closely to exact values despite the loop through grind_size -> extraction -> bitter/sour/watery and freshness -> watery.

variable_elimination.note: Exact posteriors for all unobserved variables. Over-extraction dominates at ~0.89 because it strongly explains bitter=yes (0.85), sour=no (0.90), and watery=no. Fine grind rises to ~0.57 as the most likely grind setting causing over-extraction.
